topic.what: Bilevel optimization framework for malware detection that models iterative adversarial co-evolution between defender and attacker
topic.how: Uses iterative best-response dynamics where defender retrains on cumulative adversarial samples and attacker adapts strategies each round; MAB-malware generates perturbations
topic.why_matters: Standard ML classifiers and one-shot adversarial training fail against adaptive attackers with evasion rates 77-96%; need strategic co-adaptation for robust defense

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of malware samples successfully bypassing detector under MAB-malware attack
characteristics.vs_baseline: Baseline: 77-96% ER, Bilevel: 0-1.89% ER; two orders of magnitude improvement
characteristics.evidence: Tables 1-3 across Mokes Strab DCRat families
characteristics.context: Best result achieved for Mokes/DCRat at 0%; marginal 1.89% for Strab

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average number of MAB-malware queries required for successful evasion
characteristics.vs_baseline: Strab: 31 queries baseline to 3118 bilevel (100x increase)
characteristics.context: MAB-malware query count as proxy for attacker operational cost

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of bilevel iterations required to reach epsilon-Nash equilibrium
characteristics.vs_baseline: Mokes:4 iters DCRat:3 iters Strab:5 iters
characteristics.context: Rapid convergence; terminates when no new adversarial samples generated

characteristics.what_it_measures: Standard accuracy on clean (non-adversarial) test samples
characteristics.vs_baseline: No degradation on clean data; Strab improved from 0.90 to 0.98
characteristics.context: Hardening does not harm standard classification

characteristics.what_it_measures: Evasion rate after one-shot adversarial retraining
characteristics.vs_baseline: One-shot fails especially for DCRat (90% ER still)
characteristics.evidence: Table 3 DCRat demonstrates failure of standard retraining
characteristics.context: Single-round augmentation insufficient for diverse attack surface

characteristics.what_it_measures: Whether system reached stable equilibrium with minimal exploitability
characteristics.value: Yes for Mokes/DCRat; near-equilibrium for Strab
characteristics.vs_baseline: Attacker evasion rate drops to 0% or marginal 1.89% and stabilizes
characteristics.context: Bilevel process effectively exhausts attacker search space

novelty: Bilevel optimization formulation of defender-attacker co-evolution for malware detection
Iterative best-response dynamics approximating game-theoretic equilibrium
Demonstration that one-shot adversarial training is fundamentally insufficient
Convergence analysis showing epsilon-Nash attainment across diverse malware families
open_problems: Scaling to deep neural networks and gradient boosted trees beyond Random Forest
Evaluation across larger variety of malware families beyond three tested
Impact of different attacker reward functions on defense boundaries
Handling distribution shifts in non-stationary malware environments
